description: §6.6.10 — two V5-strict derived cells whose initializers reference each other (`const <a> = @b + 1` / `const <b> = @a + 1`) form a mutual circular derived dependency. This fires E-DERIVED-CIRCULAR-DEP as a fatal error and blocks codegen (a cyclic `_scrml_derived_subscribe` pair would otherwise ship an infinite-recompute loop to the client). Regression guard for Rx-1: the detector previously only saw the legacy `@`-form (structuralForm:false) derived cells; the V5-strict `const <x> = expr` form (structuralForm:true) was routed through the plain-state collector, so its dep edges were never built and the cycle compiled clean in the real pipeline.
rationale: §6.6.10: a derived cell whose RHS depends (directly or transitively) on a cell whose RHS depends back on it is a circular derived dependency — an infinite recompute loop. Both the legacy `@`-form and the V5-strict `const <x> = expr` form are derived cells (both emit `_scrml_derived_subscribe`), so both must participate in the derived-reads cycle subgraph. E-DERIVED-CIRCULAR-DEP is a fatal error (§34) — a fatal error blocks the build, so no cyclic derived artifact reaches the client.
